plan: 1. Create the `InferenceStatement` type in `src/modules/shadow-profile/inference-schema.ts` with required fields: `statement` (string — the inferred claim), `evidence` (array of `{ signal: string; value: string; source: string }` objects linking back to collected signals), `confidence` (union type `'low' | 'medium' | 'high'`), and `marker` (literal `'[Inference]'` — the explicit inference marker). This type enforces that every shadow profile claim carries all four acceptance-criteria fields.

2. Add a `ConfidenceLabel` type alias (`'low' | 'medium' | 'high'`) and an `EvidenceEntry` interface (`{ signal: string; value: string; source: string }`) as named sub-types so they can be reused independently by future inference-rule logic (US-025) and rendering modules.
3. Add an `INFERENCE_MARKER` constant (`'[Inference]' as const`) exported from the schema file, ensuring a single source of truth for the marker string used across rendering and tests.

4. Create a `createInference` pure factory function in the same file that accepts `{ statement, evidence, confidence }` and returns a fully-formed `InferenceStatement` with the `marker` field auto-populated from `INFERENCE_MARKER`. This prevents callers from omitting or mis-typing the marker.

5. Add a runtime validation guard `isValidInference(value: unknown): value is InferenceStatement` that checks: (a) `statement` is a non-empty string, (b) `evidence` is a non-empty array of objects each with `signal`, `value`, and `source` strings, (c) `confidence` is one of the three allowed labels, (d) `marker` equals `INFERENCE_MARKER`. This guard will be used by rendering code to reject malformed data.
6. Create barrel export at `src/modules/shadow-profile/index.ts` exporting all types, the constant, the factory, and the guard.

7. Create `tests/us-024/inference-schema.test.ts` with unit tests covering: (a) `createInference` produces an object matching `InferenceStatement`, (b) factory auto-sets marker to `'[Inference]'`, (c) `isValidInference` accepts valid inference objects, (d) `isValidInference` rejects objects missing any required field, (e) `isValidInference` rejects empty evidence arrays, (f) `isValidInference` rejects invalid confidence labels, (g) `isValidInference` rejects wrong marker values.
8. Run `npm run lint`, `npm run typecheck`, and `npm run test` to verify all checks pass.
testing_recommendations: Unit test `createInference` returns an object with all four required fields (`statement`, `evidence`, `confidence`, `marker`) and that `marker` is always `'[Inference]'`
Unit test `isValidInference` returns `true` for a well-formed inference object
Unit test `isValidInference` returns `false` when `statement` is empty or missing
Unit test `isValidInference` returns `false` when `evidence` is an empty array or missing
Unit test `isValidInference` returns `false` when `confidence` is not one of `'low' | 'medium' | 'high'`
Unit test `isValidInference` returns `false` when `marker` is missing or not `'[Inference]'`
Unit test `isValidInference` rejects evidence entries missing `signal`, `value`, or `source` fields
Run `npm run typecheck` to confirm the exported types compile without errors and integrate with the existing `SignalSnapshot` type from US-009
risks: Over-engineering the schema before inference rules (US-025) are designed — keep the type minimal and extend later if needed
The `marker` field as a literal type may feel redundant at the type level but is necessary for runtime validation and rendering guarantees
No risk to existing modules — this ticket is purely additive with no changes to existing files beyond the new barrel export
